metadata.scheme_name: Merit cum Means Scholarship: Engineering University

raw_data.details: "Merit cum Means Scholarship: Engineering University" is a scholarship scheme by the Technical Education Department, Government of Chhattisgarh. The scheme provides ₹1,000 per month to eligible students for pursuing education from recognized engineering universities in Chhattisgarh. To be eligible, the student must have passed the 10+2 (or equivalent 12th grade) examination from any recognised institution located in Chhattisgarh, with a minimum of 45% marks. The total annual income of the student's father/guardian should not exceed ₹2,50,000.
raw_data.eligibility: The applicant must be a native resident of Chhattisgarh. The applicant must be a Student. The applicant must be currently studying in a recognised engineering university in Chhattisgarh. The applicant must have passed the 10+2 (or equivalent 12th grade) examination from any recognised institution located in Chhattisgarh, with a minimum of 45% marks. The total annual income of the student's father/guardian should not exceed ₹2,50,000. OTHER CONDITIONSIn addition to students studying in a recognised government educational institution located in Chhattisgarh, scholarships can be given in a limited number, based on merit, for regular study in BE/BTech courses in institutions outside the state approved by AICTE. Students regularly studying in institutions operated by a recognised university within the same campus will also be eligible for scholarships, even if they do not require AICTE approval. As per the provision made in rule 1, these scholarships will be given to eligible students for degree courses in engineering in national importance institutions of the country such as All India Institutes of Technology (IITs) and other higher technical institutions operated by the government (Central or State government) such as (NITs) and science education institutions such as (IISc), even if they do not require AICTE approval. Without special approval from the government, no student will receive more than one scholarship at the same time. However, if the institution where he is studying has given him any fee concession, then he will be entitled to continue receiving it. If any scholarship becomes vacant during its duration, then the approving authority can give such scholarships for its unexpired period to the next eligible and deserving candidate from the merit list. Only those students will be eligible to receive scholarships who have passed the qualifying examination from any testing body of Chhattisgarh. But children of such permanent government employees of Chhattisgarh can be exempted from this condition who are on deputation to the Government of India or any other state government. Such students will not be eligible to receive scholarships who are in full-time employment. In this regard, only one concession can be given that any such student who is regularly studying in a recognised institution and is receiving any scholarship can be allowed to do part-time work. In special cases not covered under the provisions of these rules, the government will provide special scholarships to eligible students at the rate prescribed for each course of study, based on poverty and merit. The total annual expenditure incurred in this regard will not exceed the budget limit.
raw_data.application_process:
Offline
Step 1: The interested applicant should visit the Office of the Principal of the Institution and request a hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the institute staff.
Step 2: In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
Step 3: Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents, within the prescribed period (if any), to the staff. The Principal will verify the documents and forward the application to the Directorate for approval (New applications) or process the renewal (for existing beneficiaries).
Step 4: Request a receipt or acknowledgement from the staff to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).
raw_data.documents_required: Residential Certificate of Chhattisgarh. Mark sheet of the Qualifying Examination. Proof of Admission/Enrolment into the Engineering University.

The scheme provides ₹1,000 per month to eligible students for pursuing education from recognized engineering universities in Chhattisgarh. To be eligible, the student must have passed the 10+2 (or equivalent 12th grade) examination from any recognised institution located in Chhattisgarh, with a minimum of 45% marks. The total annual income of the student's father/guardian should not exceed ₹2,50,000. BenefitsScholarship of ₹1,000 per month. EligibilityThe applicant must be a native resident of Chhattisgarh. The applicant must be a Student. The applicant must be currently studying in a recognised engineering university in Chhattisgarh. The total annual income of the student's father/guardian should not exceed ₹2,50,000. OTHER CONDITIONSIn addition to students studying in a recognised government educational institution located in Chhattisgarh, scholarships can be given in a limited number, based on merit, for regular study in BE/BTech courses in institutions outside the state approved by AICTE. Students regularly studying in institutions operated by a recognised university within the same campus will also be eligible for scholarships, even if they do not require AICTE approval. As per the provision made in rule 1, these scholarships will be given to eligible students for degree courses in engineering in national importance institutions of the country such as All India Institutes of Technology (IITs) and other higher technical institutions operated by the government (Central or State government) such as (NITs) and science education institutions such as (IISc), even if they do not require AICTE approval. Without special approval from the government, no student will receive more than one scholarship at the same time. However, if the institution where he is studying has given him any fee concession, then he will be entitled to continue receiving it. If any scholarship becomes vacant during its duration, then the approving authority can give such scholarships for its unexpired period to the next eligible and deserving candidate from the merit list. Only those students will be eligible to receive scholarships who have passed the qualifying examination from any testing body of Chhattisgarh. But children of such permanent government employees of Chhattisgarh can be exempted from this condition who are on deputation to the Government of India or any other state government. In this regard, only one concession can be given that any such student who is regularly studying in a recognised institution and is receiving any scholarship can be allowed to do part-time work. In special cases not covered under the provisions of these rules, the government will provide special scholarships to eligible students at the rate prescribed for each course of study, based on poverty and merit. The total annual expenditure incurred in this regard will not exceed the budget limit. Application ProcessOfflineStep 1: The interested applicant should visit the Office of the Principal of the Institution and request a hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the institute staff. Step 2: In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required). Step 3: Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents, within the prescribed period (if any), to the staff. The Principal will verify the documents and forward the application to the Directorate for approval (New applications) or process the renewal (for existing beneficiaries). Step 4: Request a receipt or acknowledgement from the staff to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable). Frequently Asked QuestionsWhat are the residency requirements to be eligible for this engineering scholarship? The applicant must be a native resident of Chhattisgarh and must have passed the 10+2 (or equivalent) examination from a recognized institution located in Chhattisgarh. Who is eligible to apply for this monthly scholarship for engineering studies? Students currently studying in a recognized engineering university in Chhattisgarh or certain approved institutions outside the state can apply. What is the minimum academic requirement to qualify for this scholarship? The applicant must have secured at least 45% marks in the 10+2 (or equivalent) examination from a recognized institution in Chhattisgarh. What is the maximum annual family income allowed for applicants to be eligible? The total annual income of the student’s father or guardian should not exceed ₹2,50,000. How much financial assistance is provided under this scholarship scheme? Eligible students receive ₹1,000 per month as a scholarship for their engineering studies. Can students studying in private engineering colleges in Chhattisgarh apply for this scholarship? Yes, if the college is a recognized engineering university or institution approved by the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE). Are students studying in engineering institutions outside Chhattisgarh eligible for this scholarship? Yes, but only in limited numbers for BE/BTech courses in AICTE-approved institutions outside the state, based on merit. Do students enrolled in government engineering colleges in Chhattisgarh qualify for this scholarship? Yes, students studying in recognized government or private engineering institutions in Chhattisgarh are eligible. Are students studying in Indian Institutes of Technology (IITs) eligible for this scholarship? Yes, students enrolled in national importance institutions like IITs, National Institutes of Technology (NITs), or Indian Institute of Science (IISc) are eligible. Can a student receive this scholarship along with another scholarship simultaneously? No, without special government approval, a student cannot receive more than one scholarship at the same time. If a student gets a fee concession from their institution, can they still receive this scholarship? Yes, students receiving fee concessions from their institution can still be eligible for this scholarship. What happens if a scholarship becomes vacant during the academic year? The vacant scholarship may be awarded to the next eligible candidate from the merit list for the remaining duration. Are children of Chhattisgarh government employees on deputation eligible for this scholarship? Yes, children of permanent Chhattisgarh government employees on deputation to other states or the central government are exempt from the qualifying exam condition. Can working professionals pursuing engineering studies apply for this scholarship? No, students in full-time employment are not eligible, but part-time work may be allowed for those already receiving a scholarship. Are there any special provisions for economically weak but meritorious students not covered under normal rules? Yes, the government may provide special scholarships based on poverty and merit, within the prescribed budget limits. What documents are required to apply for this engineering scholarship? Applicants must submit a Chhattisgarh residential certificate, qualifying exam mark sheet, and proof of admission/enrollment. raw_data.scheme_name: Merit cum Means Scholarship: Engineering University

faqs.question: What are the residency requirements to be eligible for this engineering scholarship?
faqs.answer: The applicant must be a native resident of Chhattisgarh and must have passed the 10+2 (or equivalent) examination from a recognized institution located in Chhattisgarh.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for this monthly scholarship for engineering studies?
faqs.answer: Students currently studying in a recognized engineering university in Chhattisgarh or certain approved institutions outside the state can apply.

faqs.question: What is the minimum academic requirement to qualify for this scholarship?
faqs.answer: The applicant must have secured at least 45% marks in the 10+2 (or equivalent) examination from a recognized institution in Chhattisgarh.

faqs.question: What is the maximum annual family income allowed for applicants to be eligible?
faqs.answer: The total annual income of the student’s father or guardian should not exceed ₹2,50,000.

faqs.question: How much financial assistance is provided under this scholarship scheme?
faqs.answer: Eligible students receive ₹1,000 per month as a scholarship for their engineering studies.

faqs.question: Can students studying in private engineering colleges in Chhattisgarh apply for this scholarship?
faqs.answer: Yes, if the college is a recognized engineering university or institution approved by the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE).

faqs.question: Are students studying in engineering institutions outside Chhattisgarh eligible for this scholarship?
faqs.answer: Yes, but only in limited numbers for BE/BTech courses in AICTE-approved institutions outside the state, based on merit.

faqs.question: Do students enrolled in government engineering colleges in Chhattisgarh qualify for this scholarship?
faqs.answer: Yes, students studying in recognized government or private engineering institutions in Chhattisgarh are eligible.

faqs.question: Are students studying in Indian Institutes of Technology (IITs) eligible for this scholarship?
faqs.answer: Yes, students enrolled in national importance institutions like IITs, National Institutes of Technology (NITs), or Indian Institute of Science (IISc) are eligible.

faqs.question: Can a student receive this scholarship along with another scholarship simultaneously?
faqs.answer: No, without special government approval, a student cannot receive more than one scholarship at the same time.

faqs.question: If a student gets a fee concession from their institution, can they still receive this scholarship?
faqs.answer: Yes, students receiving fee concessions from their institution can still be eligible for this scholarship.

faqs.question: What happens if a scholarship becomes vacant during the academic year?
faqs.answer: The vacant scholarship may be awarded to the next eligible candidate from the merit list for the remaining duration.

faqs.question: Are children of Chhattisgarh government employees on deputation eligible for this scholarship?
faqs.answer: Yes, children of permanent Chhattisgarh government employees on deputation to other states or the central government are exempt from the qualifying exam condition.

faqs.question: Can working professionals pursuing engineering studies apply for this scholarship?
faqs.answer: No, students in full-time employment are not eligible, but part-time work may be allowed for those already receiving a scholarship.

faqs.question: Are there any special provisions for economically weak but meritorious students not covered under normal rules?
faqs.answer: Yes, the government may provide special scholarships based on poverty and merit, within the prescribed budget limits.

faqs.question: What documents are required to apply for this engineering scholarship?
faqs.answer: Applicants must submit a Chhattisgarh residential certificate, qualifying exam mark sheet, and proof of admission/enrollment.
